step_by_step_reasoning: The speaker explains how Lagoon deploy platform works. A developer pushes code to Git. A webhook triggers Lagoon EKS, which manages deployment. Lagoon provisions the application inside target EKS clusters. These cluster workloads run on standard EC2 or EC2 Spot instances. The platform uses Aurora R6g, EBS GP3, and EFS for persistent storage depending on the workload type.

graph.name: Amazie.io Lagoon Kubernetes Delivery Platform
graph.notes: Application delivery platform leveraging EKS, EC2 Spot, and ARM-based Aurora R6g instances to reduce costs by 70-90% and improve I/O performance.

nodes.name: Developer
nodes.notes: Pushes code to trigger Git-based workflow.

nodes.name: Git Repository
nodes.notes: Hosts application code and triggers Lagoon via webhook.

nodes.name: Lagoon EKS
nodes.notes: Core Lagoon application platform handling orchestration.

nodes.name: Target EKS
nodes.notes: Target Kubernetes cluster containing developer environments.

nodes.name: EC2 Spot
nodes.notes: Used for non-production environments to save up to 90% cost.

nodes.name: Aurora R6g
nodes.notes: Database storage utilizing Graviton2 (R6g) ARM instances.

nodes.name: EBS GP3
nodes.notes: Block storage for databases and search workloads like OpenSearch.

nodes.name: EFS
nodes.notes: Shared file system for standard application assets.
